items.alternative: This unit should be assessed as NOT self-contained. The text begins with 'وشروح هذا الكتاب' — the demonstrative 'هذا الكتاب' refers to a book (the Alfiyya) not identified within the unit itself. Additionally, the phrase 'هذه الكلمة الموجزة' refers to a broader introduction/preface whose context is external. The text is clearly a fragment of a longer muqaddima (introduction), listing commentators on a work whose identity must be inferred from outside the unit. It also ends abruptly mid-sentence with 'أحد علماء القرن' — the century number is cut off, violating C-SC-4 (completeness). While the general topic (listing commentaries on the Alfiyya) can be guessed, the unit fails C-SC-2 (unresolved demonstratives) and C-SC-4 (incomplete text).
items.reasoning: The decision says PARTIAL, meaning partially self-contained. However, I think the unit fails more significantly than 'partial' suggests. The opening 'وشروح هذا الكتاب' has an unresolved demonstrative — 'هذا الكتاب' is not identified within the unit. The text is clearly a section from a muqaddima/introduction to a sharh, and it ends mid-sentence ('أحد علماء القرن' with the century number cut off). These are clear violations of C-SC-2 and C-SC-4. While the general idea is understandable (listing famous commentaries on the Alfiyya), the assessment of PARTIAL seems too generous — it should lean more toward NOT self-contained given the unresolved reference at the very start and the truncated ending.
